# Help is the discoverability contract. help_text.txt pins the rich
# sections (cron syntax, behaviour matrix). This file pins the *forms*
# — version flags, the `help` subcommand, the -h short flag, and a
# smoke probe for every remaining subcommand's --help.

# --version / -v.
exec eon --version
stdout 'version'
exec eon -v
stdout 'version'

# `eon help` with no argument shows the top-level overview.
exec eon help
stdout 'cron-style'
stdout 'add '

# `eon help <sub>` mirrors `eon <sub> --help`.
exec eon help add
stdout 'CRON SYNTAX'
exec eon help ls
stdout '--kind'
exec eon help logs
stdout 'Behaviour matrix'

# -h short flag must work for the root.
exec eon -h
stdout 'cron-style'

# Every subcommand must respond to --help with non-empty output.
# We don't pin the specific contents — that would duplicate
# help_text.txt — only that the path doesn't crash and yields
# at least the USAGE block.
exec eon show --help
stdout 'USAGE'
exec eon rm --help
stdout 'USAGE'
exec eon prune --help
stdout 'USAGE'
exec eon enable --help
stdout 'USAGE'
exec eon disable --help
stdout 'USAGE'
exec eon status --help
stdout 'USAGE'
exec eon install --help
stdout 'USAGE'
exec eon uninstall --help
stdout 'USAGE'
exec eon stop --help
stdout 'USAGE'
exec eon seppuku --help
stdout 'USAGE'

# Nested subcommand help.
exec eon debug --help
stdout 'USAGE'
exec eon debug db --help
stdout 'USAGE'
